rows.item: 学習意欲・授業参加度

summary:
Have you travelled anywhere? Are you interested in the tourism industry?
By looking at different elements of travel culture that are related to the English language, this course allows for the development of English skills in a realistic and entertaining setting.
aims: This is a four skills (reading, writing, listening, speaking) English language course. Lessons are related to the theme of travel and will introduce a variety of useful English skills including vocabulary, grammar constructions, thematic writing, and creative expression. The skills which are introduced will be practiced in two student projects.
goals: Students will learn English grammar and vocabulary
Students will learn creative and expressive techniques in English
Students will learn about travel and tourism through materials in English

plan.text:
[Week 1. Introduction - Course overview]
Introduction and overview of the course, assessments and other requirements. Meet your classmates and teacher.

[Week 1. Introduction - Class blog]
Introduction of the class blog and how to use this.

[Week 2. Travel blogging - The language of social media]
How do we communicate on social media? In this lesson we will learn about the vocabulary, slang and even emojis used for online communication.

[Week 2. Travel blogging - Simple past tense]
When writing about completed actions in the past, we use the simple past tense. In this lesson we will write about things that we've experienced in recent times.

[Week 3. Sightseeing at attractions - Phrasal verbs]
When we go sightseeing, we "get out" of the house and "check out" interesting things around us.

[Week 3. Sightseeing at attractions - Superlative adjectives]
Superlative adjectives allow us to describe an extreme example of something. What is the best attraction you have ever visited? What is the worst?

[Week 4. Packing and planning - Simple future tense]
What are you going to pack for your trip? In this lesson, we'll use the simple future tense to talk about things we plan to do.

[Week 4. Packing and planning - Time and conditional clauses]
We'll use time and conditional clauses to plan for an imaginary trip.

[Week 5 - History and culture - Passive voice with by]
We'll talk about things that were done in the past and who did them.

[Week 5 - History and culture - Indefinite pronouns]
We can use indefinite pronouns to talk about groups of people, places or things. In this lesson, we'll use indefinite pronouns to describe local culture.

[Week 6. Project 1 - Overview]
This lesson will introduce our first project "A trip to a World Heritage Site" and its two components: a presentation and a language checklist.

[Week 6. Project 1 - Researching]
This lesson will introduce effective techniques for online research and will teach the skill of writing in dot points

[Week 7. Project 1 preparation - Writing in sentences and paragraphs]
Last lesson we collected information using research. In this lesson, we will look at how to organise information using sentences and paragraphs.

[Week 7. Project 1 preparation - Practice and feedback]
Students will practice their presentation in pairs and with the teacher and get individual feedback on this with the goal of improving their performance.

[Week 8. Project 1 presentations]
Students will give presentations during class time and respond to the presentations of other students.
Students will also submit their language checklists which showcase their knowledge of the content learned in Weeks 2, 3, 4, and 5.

[Week 8. Project 1 presentations]
Students will give presentations during class time and respond to the presentations of other students.

[Week 9. Writing a review - Adjectives]
A review tells us people what we thought about a particular thing or experience. In this lesson, we'll use adjectives to write about experiences we had in detail.

[Week 9. Writing a review - Modifiers]
Modifiers make the meaning of a word more specific. In this lesson, we'll use modifiers to make the writing in our reviews more accurate and informative.

[Week 10. Tourists and travellers - Comparisons using as...as]
Tourists are not as adventurous as travellers. In this lesson we will learn the grammar to compare two different groups.

[Week 10. Tourists and travellers - Comparative adjectives]
In this lesson we will learn how to make comparisons using adjectives.

[Week 11. Meeting people - Icebreakers]
One of the most exciting things about travel is meeting new people and communicating with them. This lesson will look at how to start conversations with strangers.

[Week 11. Meeting people - Modals of advice]
This lesson will look at the vocabulary used to offer advice.

[Week 12. The tourism industry - Subject verb agreement]
The tourism industry is the world's largest industry and has a fascinating history. While looking at the tourism industry, this lesson will look at when to pluralize verbs.

[Week 12. The tourism industry - Plurals]
This lesson will focus on when to add an "s" to nouns to make the plural form.

[Week 13. Project 2 - Overview]
This lesson will introduce our second project "A tour of _____" and its two components: a presentation and a language checklist.

[Week 13. Project 2 -Storyboard]
This lesson will introduce the idea of the storyboard which allows us to organise ideas and plan in a visually-oriented way.

[Week 14. Project 2 preparation - Proofreading]
This lesson will introduce effective techniques for checking and correcting your writing in English focusing on capitalisation, spelling, and sentence structure.

[Week 14. Project 2 preparation - Feedback and practice]
Students will practice their presentation in pairs and with the teacher and get individual feedback on this with the goal of improving their performance.

[Week 15 - Project 2 presentations]
Students will give presentations during class time and respond to the presentations of other students.
Students will also submit their language checklists which showcase their knowledge of the content learned in Weeks 9, 10, 11, and 12.

[Week 15 - Project 2 presentations]
Students will give presentations during class time and respond to the presentations of other students.

[Week 16. Final mini test]
Students will review course content in preparation for a classroom test.
[Week 16. Final mini test]

textbooks: Materials will be provided in class
prereq:
-Students should have an interest in domestic or foreign travel.
-Students should be willing to participate in class activities including spoken activities and presentations.
-Students must complete any online class content including participating in the class blog.
-Students must submit all assessments, including spoken presentations, essays, blog posts, etc., before the end of the last lesson (unless there is a written agreement with the teacher stating that the student can submit at a later date). If all assessment tasks are not completed, the student will not be able to pass the course.
-Students must not be absent for more than 1/3 of total classes.
prep: It is necessary to complete homework and preview/review lesson content.
teachers: ◎ マガシック ミケランジェロ

extra.text: 平成20年度以降入学生／令和６年度以降入学生

extra.label: 区分2

extra.label: この授業で身につける「10+1の能力」
extra.text:
人類の文化・社会・自然に関する知識
語学に関するリテラシー
コミュニケーション力
協働実践力

extra.label: 成績評価に関する補足
extra.text:
30% Project 1 (Presentation + language checklist = 20+10) PowerPoint presentation with a focus on practiced speech, competency in the learned material.
30% Project 2 (Presentation + language checklist = 20+10) PowerPoint presentation with a focus on practiced speech, competency in the learned material.
20% Class blog/Participation = Attendance and attitude, completion of activities and homework,
20% Final test = Review of content

教員の実務経験との関連
